preamble: Epenthesis of [tʃ] (ch) or [x] (ch-like) often occurs as an excrescent sound or as a prothetic development before certain glides.

examples.note: Excrescent [t] inserted between nasal [n] and fricative [ʃ] in 'bench'; however, the [t] may reflect original OE 'benc' [tʃ] rather than true insertion.
Prothesis of a velar fricative [x] (not strictly [tʃ]) before initial glide [w] in 'huevo'; the exact realization varies by dialect.
